extraction.one_line_summary: VIVIANI Elia won the bunch sprint after Deceuninck-Quick Step executed a three-man lead-out train (Asgreen-Lampaert-Morkov) in final kilometers
tactical_patterns: Bora-Hansgrohe controlled breakaway gap despite Sagan sprinting, revealing dual role strategy protecting GC and sprint ambitions simultaneously.
Deceuninck-Quick Step deployed full three-rider lead-out sequence (Asgreen-Lampaert-Morkov) suggesting confidence in Viviani's finishing speed over positioning gambles.
Frank's late solo attempt from AG2R illustrates Swiss riders prioritizing home stage wins over GC positioning logic.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Crashed at 30km to go requiring hospitalization, compounding difficult week for Ineos team morale and Tour preparation
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: First Tour de Suisse win suggests peaking schedule aligned despite five-month gap since previous victory
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: Second place finish moved him to second GC, revealing sprinter classification capability on moderately hilly terrain
